paper_title: Feeding strategies of circum‑Mediterranean hipparionins during the late Miocene: Exploring dietary preferences related to size through dental microwear analysis

methods.method_name: Sample Collection and Study Area Definition
methods.description: This method describes the collection of fossil specimens from late Miocene fossil sites around the circum-Mediterranean basins, focusing on localities with large and diverse hipparionin assemblages.

steps.description: The fossil specimens come from 20 late Miocene fossil sites of circum-Mediterranean basins.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fossil specimens from specified Miocene sites

steps.description: We divided the eastern Mediterranean Turolian sample into the western Anatolian and the Balkans bioprovinces.

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and scanning of dental replicas from hipparionin teeth to analyze microwear patterns.

steps.description: The occlusal surfaces of the teeth were cleaned using acetone-soaked cotton swabs to remove dust particles and glue.
Dental replicas were made using a polyvinyl siloxane silicone of medium consistency.
These molds were scanned with the 'TRIDENT' Leica DCM8 white-light confocal profilometer.
steps.searchable_summary: scan dental molds with confocal profilometer

steps.description: The surfaces were saved as .plμ files by LeicaScan software and imported for processing to LeicaMap.
